title: Prefer typed agent messages when extracting Codex output
context: Codex output stream includes multiple text events (reasoning, trailing events, actual response) with different semantic meaning. Previous code took any last text found, risking selection of reasoning or incomplete content instead of the actual assistant response.
decision: Parse events semantically: collect texts tagged as `agent_message` (or assistant/message variants) separately, return the last tagged agent message if available, only fall back to last-any text when no typed agent message exists.
consequences: Eliminates incorrect output selection when Codex emits reasoning/trailing text after the answer
Degrades gracefully if schema drifts (falls back to old behavior rather than crashing)
Maintains backward compatibility with untyped event formats
alternatives_rejected: Strict type-required parsing that fails on schema changes
Taking first agent message instead of last (would miss corrections/refinements)
Ignoring the type field and continuing with dumb last-text strategy
